---
title: "Overview"
---

This convention leverages Shiny's built-in module functionality to organize application files and code to make developing, updating, and collaborating on apps easier than the traditional single-file method.
These modules are not to be confused with Python's native modules, these are designed specifically for the Shiny framework.

Each UI layout has top-level navigation tabs (either in a navbar or a sidebar) to allow the user to navigate the various pages within the application.
For each navigation tab, there will be a separate Shiny module and each module will have its own separate R file.
Each file will contain only its associated page's UI and server code.

More here: [Shiny Modules](https://shiny.posit.co/r/articles/improve/modules/)
